context: This chapter describes the path to moksha (liberation from the cycle of birth and death). It expounds on devotion to Vishnu/Narayana, the knowledge of Brahman, the various means to attain liberation, and the blissful state of the liberated soul that merges with the Supreme.

verses.hindi:
गरुड उवाच —
मोक्षधर्मं समाचक्ष्व भगवन् परमेश्वर।
येन मुक्तो भवेज्जन्तुः संसाराद् दुःखसागरात्॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuḍa uvāca —
Mokṣadharmaṁ samācakṣva Bhagavan Parameśvara,
yena mukto bhavej jantuḥ saṁsārād duḥkhasāgarāt.
verses.translation: Garuda said: O Blessed Lord, Supreme God, describe the dharma of liberation, by which a living being becomes freed from the ocean of sorrow that is worldly existence.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
शृणु तार्क्ष्य प्रवक्ष्यामि मोक्षमार्गं सनातनम्।
ज्ञानभक्तिविरक्त्या च मुक्तिर्भवति देहिनाम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
Śṛṇu Tārkṣya pravakṣyāmi mokṣamārgaṁ sanātanam,
jñānabhaktiviraktyā ca muktir bhavati dehinām.
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: Listen, O Tarshkya, I shall describe the eternal path of liberation. Through knowledge, devotion, and detachment, liberation comes to embodied beings.

verses.hindi:
ज्ञानेन मुक्तिमाप्नोति ज्ञानं ध्यानेन लभ्यते।
ध्यानं शीलवतां नित्यं गुरुसेवापरायणाम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Jñānena muktim āpnoti jñānaṁ dhyānena labhyate,
dhyānaṁ śīlavatāṁ nityaṁ gurusevāparāyaṇām.
verses.translation: Through knowledge one attains liberation; knowledge is obtained through meditation. Meditation is the constant practice of the virtuous who are devoted to the service of the guru.

verses.hindi:
अहं ब्रह्मास्मि इत्येवं यो जानाति स मुच्यते।
आत्मानमात्मना पश्यन् सर्वत्र समदर्शनः॥
verses.transliteration:
Ahaṁ Brahmāsmi ity evaṁ yo jānāti sa mucyate,
ātmānam ātmanā paśyan sarvatra samadarśanaḥ.
verses.translation: He who knows 'I am Brahman' is liberated. Seeing the Self by the self, he has equal vision everywhere.

verses.hindi:
ब्रह्म सत्यं जगन्मिथ्या जीवो ब्रह्मैव नापरः।
इति वेदान्तवाक्येन मुच्यते भवबन्धनात्॥
verses.transliteration:
Brahma satyaṁ jagan mithyā jīvo Brahmaiva nāparaḥ,
iti Vedāntavākyena mucyate bhavabandhanāt.
verses.translation: Brahman is real, the world is illusory, and the individual soul is none other than Brahman. By this declaration of Vedanta, one is freed from the bondage of worldly existence.

verses.hindi:
न कर्मणा न प्रजया धनेन
त्यागेनैके अमृतत्वमानशुः।
सर्वं परित्यज्य हि ब्रह्मविद्यां
प्राप्नोति यः सोऽमृतत्वं च धत्ते॥
verses.transliteration:
Na karmaṇā na prajayā dhanena
tyāgenaike amṛtatvam ānaśuḥ,
sarvaṁ parityajya hi brahmavidyāṁ
prāpnoti yaḥ so'mṛtatvaṁ ca dhatte.
verses.translation: Not by rituals, not by offspring, not by wealth — but by renunciation alone have some attained immortality. He who, renouncing all, attains the knowledge of Brahman, holds immortality.

verses.hindi:
भक्त्या त्वनन्यया शक्य अहमेवंविधोऽर्जुन।
ज्ञातुं द्रष्टुं च तत्त्वेन प्रवेष्टुं च परन्तप॥
verses.transliteration:
Bhaktyā tv ananyayā śakya aham evaṁvidho'rjuna,
jñātuṁ draṣṭuṁ ca tattvena praveṣṭuṁ ca parantapa.
verses.translation: By single-minded devotion alone can I be known, seen in truth, and entered into, O scorcher of foes — thus spoke the Lord.

verses.hindi:
नारायणं परं ब्रह्म नारायणं परं तपः।
नारायणं परं ज्योतिर्नारायणं परायणम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Nārāyaṇaṁ paraṁ Brahma Nārāyaṇaṁ paraṁ tapaḥ,
Nārāyaṇaṁ paraṁ jyotir Nārāyaṇaṁ parāyaṇam.
verses.translation: Narayana is the Supreme Brahman, Narayana is the supreme austerity, Narayana is the supreme light, Narayana is the supreme refuge.

verses.hindi:
ये तु नारायणं देवं भक्त्या परमया युताः।
नमन्ति सततं भक्ता ते मुक्ताः सर्वबन्धनैः॥
verses.transliteration:
Ye tu Nārāyaṇaṁ devaṁ bhaktyā paramayā yutāḥ,
namanti satataṁ bhaktā te muktāḥ sarvabandhaiḥ.
verses.translation: Those devotees who, endowed with supreme devotion, constantly bow to Lord Narayana — they are freed from all bondage.

verses.hindi:
विष्णोर्नामसहस्रं यः सदा संकीर्तयेन् नरः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥
verses.transliteration:
Viṣṇor nāmasahasraṁ yaḥ sadā saṁkīrtayen naraḥ,
sarvapāpavinirmukto Viṣṇulokaṁ sa gacchati.
verses.translation: He who always chants the thousand names of Vishnu, freed from all sins, goes to the abode of Vishnu.

verses.hindi:
सकृत् उच्चरितं येन हरिरित्यक्षरद्वयम्।
बद्धः परिकरस्तेन मोक्षाय गमनं प्रति॥
verses.transliteration:
Sakṛt uccaritaṁ yena Harir ity akṣaradvayam,
baddhaḥ parikaras tena mokṣāya gamanaṁ prati.
verses.translation: He who has uttered even once the two syllables 'Hari' has already girded himself for the journey to liberation.

verses.hindi:
अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन् मुक्त्वा कलेबरम्।
यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः॥
verses.transliteration:
Antakāle ca mām eva smaran muktvā kalebaram,
yaḥ prayāti sa madbhāvaṁ yāti nāsty atra saṁśayaḥ.
verses.translation: He who, at the time of death, remembering Me alone, departs leaving the body — he attains My nature. Of this there is no doubt.

verses.hindi:
सर्वधर्मान् परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।
अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥
verses.transliteration:
Sarvadharman parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja,
ahaṁ tvā sarvapāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ.
verses.translation: Abandoning all duties, take refuge in Me alone. I shall liberate you from all sins — do not grieve.

verses.hindi:
मुक्तात्मा ब्रह्मणा सार्धं आनन्दं परमं गतः।
न तत्र सूर्यो भाति न शशाङ्को न पावकः
यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम॥
verses.transliteration:
Muktātmā Brahmaṇā sārdham ānandaṁ paramaṁ gataḥ,
na tatra sūryo bhāti na śaśāṅko na pāvakaḥ
yad gatvā na nivartante tad dhāma paramaṁ mama.
verses.translation: The liberated soul, united with Brahman, attains supreme bliss. There neither the sun shines, nor the moon, nor fire. Having reached that supreme abode of Mine, they do not return.

verses.hindi:
आनन्दमयं ब्रह्म सच्चिदानन्दलक्षणम्।
तत्प्राप्य परमं धाम मुक्तो नावर्तते पुनः॥
verses.transliteration:
Ānandamayaṁ Brahma saccidānandalakṣaṇam,
tat prāpya paramaṁ dhāma mukto nāvartate punaḥ.
verses.translation: Brahman is full of bliss, characterized by existence, consciousness, and bliss (sat-chit-ananda). Having attained that supreme abode, the liberated one does not return again.

verses.hindi:
न तत्र भयं किञ्चिन् न शोको न जरा मृतिः।
आनन्दैकरसं सर्वं ब्रह्मभूतस्य योगिनः॥
verses.transliteration:
Na tatra bhayaṁ kiñcin na śoko na jarā mṛtiḥ,
ānandaikarasaṁ sarvaṁ Brahmabhūtasya yoginaḥ.
verses.translation: There is no fear there, no sorrow, no old age, no death. Everything is the single essence of bliss for the yogi who has become one with Brahman.

verses.hindi:
ज्ञानयोगेन साङ्ख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम्।
भक्तियोगेन भक्तानां मुक्तिर्भवति देहिनाम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Jñānayogena sāṅkhyānāṁ karmayogena yoginām,
bhaktiyogena bhaktānāṁ muktir bhavati dehinām.
verses.translation: By the yoga of knowledge for the followers of Sankhya, by the yoga of action for the yogis, and by the yoga of devotion for the devotees — liberation comes to embodied beings.

verses.hindi:
श्रवणं कीर्तनं विष्णोः स्मरणं पादसेवनम्।
अर्चनं वन्दनं दास्यं सख्यमात्मनिवेदनम्॥
verses.transliteration:
Śravaṇaṁ kīrtanaṁ Viṣṇoḥ smaraṇaṁ pādasevanam,
arcanaṁ vandanaṁ dāsyaṁ sakhyam ātmanivedanam.
verses.translation: Hearing, chanting, and remembering Vishnu, serving His feet, worshipping, bowing, serving, befriending, and surrendering the self — these are the nine forms of devotion.

verses.hindi:
एतद् विद्याद् विमुक्तात्मा सर्वसङ्गविवर्जितः।
समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः शान्तो मुक्तो न लिप्यते॥
verses.transliteration:
Etad vidyād vimuktātmā sarvasaṅgavivarjitaḥ,
samaloṣṭāśmakāñcanaḥ śānto mukto na lipyate.
verses.translation: Know the liberated soul as one who is free from all attachments, to whom a clod of earth, a stone, and gold are equal — serene, liberated, and untainted.

verses.hindi:
गरुडपुराणं श्रुत्वा यः श्रद्धया परमान्वितः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुसायुज्यमाप्नुयात्॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuḍapurāṇaṁ śrutvā yaḥ śraddhayā paramānvitaḥ,
sarvapāpavinirmukto Viṣṇusāyujyam āpnuyāt.
verses.translation: He who hears the Garuda Purana with supreme faith, freed from all sins, attains union with Vishnu.

verses.hindi:
तस्मात् सर्वप्रयत्नेन नारायणपरायणः।
भक्त्या ज्ञानेन वैराग्यैर्मोक्षमार्गं समाश्रयेत्॥
verses.transliteration:
Tasmāt sarvaprayatnena Nārāyaṇaparāyaṇaḥ,
bhaktyā jñānena vairāgyair mokṣamārgaṁ samāśrayed.
verses.translation: Therefore, with every effort, devoted to Narayana, through devotion, knowledge, and detachment, one should take refuge in the path of liberation.

verses.hindi:
इति श्रीगरुडपुराणे सारोद्धारे मोक्षवर्णनं
नाम पञ्चदशोऽध्यायः॥
verses.transliteration:
Iti Śrī Garuḍa Purāṇe Sāroddhāre Mokṣavarṇanaṁ
nāma pañcadaśo'dhyāyaḥ.
verses.translation: Thus ends the fifteenth chapter, entitled 'The Description of Liberation,' in the Saroddhara of the Shri Garuda Purana.
